entries.description: Build active-ball possession episodes and possession anchors for the perspective team.
entries.unknownBehavior: Catalog declares missing_data_semantics=unknown; implementation mainly omits segments when source frames do not qualify.
limitations: Hidden parameter dependencies are not declared on the catalog entry.
Uses canonical/raw ball possession state; does not infer control quality.
entries.visibilityNote: Recommended AGENT_COMPOSABLE after catalog parameters such as minimum_possession_seconds and analysis_rate_hz are made explicit; existing reviewed recipes may continue supplying them at recipe level.

entries.description: Normalize absolute ball y position by pitch half-width.
entries.unknownBehavior: Catalog declares unknown; implementation emits numeric values from canonical ball frames and has no explicit UNKNOWN branch.
limitations: This is a geometric kernel operation, not a football concept by itself.
Name is accurate, but should normally be lowered from ball-side or wide-entry concepts.
entries.visibilityNote: Useful lowering signal, but not normal user-facing recipe vocabulary.

entries.description: Compute defending outfield-player centroid y without goalkeeper bias.
entries.unknownBehavior: FrameSignal supports UNKNOWN via None values when centroid data is missing.
limitations: Centroid is inspectable but can overstate block shape.
Should be an internal lowering operation for block-movement concepts except in expert/debug mode.
entries.visibilityNote: Compiler/debug lowering for block-shape calculations; centroid alone can overstate tactical meaning.

entries.description: Find wide-entry candidates and measure maximum defending centroid movement toward the ball side.
entries.unknownBehavior: Catalog declares unknown; implementation skips candidates with empty baseline/search data rather than returning explicit UNKNOWN anchors.
limitations: Name sounds like a pure measure, but implementation also embeds wide-entry candidate extraction, baseline/search-window policy, quality gating, and anchor creation.
Several implementation parameters are read from global recipe parameters rather than declared on the catalog entry.
Keep TRUSTED_RECIPE_ONLY until complete dependencies are formalized.
entries.visibilityNote: Restricted because it is a high-level detector stage with undeclared window, quality, and anchor-selection dependencies.

entries.description: Classify post-anchor M1 outcomes as SWITCHED, RETAINED_NO_SWITCH, LOST_BEFORE_SWITCH, or STOPPAGE.
entries.unknownBehavior: Catalog declares unknown; current implementation classifies eligible records and emits near misses, with limited explicit UNKNOWN.
limitations: This is recipe-specific L4 logic registered as a primitive.
Boundary enforcement prevents model-authored plans from using it except via trusted host-loaded M1.
Keep TRUSTED_RECIPE_ONLY until complete dependencies are formalized.
entries.visibilityNote: Restricted because it is M1 recipe-specific bundled outcome logic.

entries.description: For a selected relation episode, classify whether the ball enters the relation destination region within a horizon.
entries.unknownBehavior: Catalog declares unknown; implementation emits deterministic entered/not-entered classifications for selected relation episodes.
limitations: Does not prove a pass was made or should have been made.
Episode selection is deterministic but narrow: first_by_duration_clearance.
entries.visibilityNote: Composable only as geometric destination-entry evidence, not pass usage or intent.

entries.description: Represent a geometrically clear forward connection from ball location to an attacking teammate over an interval.
inputs: anchor frame signal or source results
entries.unknownBehavior: Anchor coverage emits PASS, FAIL, or UNKNOWN using source availability and mixed evidence semantics.
limitations: No pass probability, receiver body orientation, offside model, intent, or optimality.
L1 geometry is embedded in relation implementation rather than expressed through cataloged kernel ops.
entries.visibilityNote: Composable relation, while internal distance/lane/bounds kernels remain compiler-lowering details.

entries.description: Same geometric corridor relation as geometric_progressive_corridor, but anchored from generic anchor_ref episode sets.
entries.unknownBehavior: Same PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN anchor coverage as geometric_progressive_corridor.
limitations: Duplicates the corridor relation under a second input contract.
The distinction is pragmatic but should be documented as input-shape adaptation, not a different football concept.
entries.visibilityNote: Composable adapter for the same relation with anchor-set input semantics.

entries.description: Canonical anchor record carrying match, period, anchor frame, window, and entity references.
entries.unknownBehavior: Invalid anchor records are rejected by runtime value conformance or ignored by runtime_anchors.
limitations: Anchor semantics depend on the producing capability.
The IR does not yet have named anchor subtypes such as regain, line-break, or support-arrival anchors.

entries.description: Per-anchor PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN coverage record for relation predicates with witness relation IDs.
entries.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN preserved when relation evidence is unavailable or mixed and no qualifying relation is proven.
limitations: This is the only current safe source for agent-visible exists/count_at_least.
Generic collection counting is intentionally not exposed to agents yet.

entries.description: Require ball to remain in a wide region long enough to avoid one-frame noise.
entries.unknownBehavior: Implemented through persists_for and embedded wide-entry candidate logic; no standalone catalog UNKNOWN behavior.
limitations: Not a current catalog entry despite an executor stub.
Should remain a lowering/predicate composition, not a standalone tactical primitive.

entries.description: Ensure a block shift is sustained rather than one noisy frame.
entries.unknownBehavior: Implemented through persists_for and legacy adapters; no standalone catalog UNKNOWN behavior.
limitations: Not a current catalog entry despite an executor stub.
Correctly represented as temporal operator lowering rather than football vocabulary.

entries.description: Describe team structure using y_95 - y_05 instead of raw extrema.
entries.unknownBehavior: No current runtime catalog behavior found.
limitations: Documented in M1 registry but absent from current runtime catalog.
Useful as internal evidence/context, not current agent vocabulary.

entries.description: Describe team structure using x_95 - x_05 instead of raw extrema.
entries.unknownBehavior: No current runtime catalog behavior found.
limitations: Documented in M1 registry but absent from current runtime catalog.
Useful as internal evidence/context, not current agent vocabulary.

entries.description: Downsample canonical 25 Hz frames to a configured analysis cadence.
entries.unknownBehavior: Raises on invalid analysis gap or cadence; not modeled as UNKNOWN.
limitations: Executor has an unregistered noop map entry named analysis_rate.
This is an execution substrate parameter, not a tactical capability.

entries.description: Strict numeric greater-than predicate.
entries.unknownBehavior: None input becomes UNKNOWN.
limitations: Should be internal compiler/lowering vocabulary except in expert/debug mode.

entries.description: Numeric greater-than-or-equal predicate.
entries.unknownBehavior: None input becomes UNKNOWN.
limitations: Should be internal compiler/lowering vocabulary except in expert/debug mode.

entries.description: Numeric less-than-or-equal predicate.
entries.unknownBehavior: None input becomes UNKNOWN.
limitations: Currently not used by shipped recipes.

entries.description: Typed equality predicate.
entries.unknownBehavior: None input becomes UNKNOWN.
limitations: Should be compiler/lowering vocabulary by default.

entries.description: Typed inequality predicate.
entries.unknownBehavior: None input becomes UNKNOWN.
limitations: Should be compiler/lowering vocabulary by default.

entries.description: Promote a boolean frame signal to PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN intervals after a minimum duration.
entries.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN remains UNKNOWN and is not converted to false.
limitations: Useful in agent-authored plans only when applied to documented football-relative frame signals.

entries.description: Test whether an anchor-relative boolean episode collection has a proven item.
entries.unknownBehavior: On anchor_evaluations, PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN is preserved from coverage records.
limitations: Agent-visible only on anchor_evaluations; raw collection existence is rejected.

entries.description: Compare an anchor-relative collection count to a threshold.
entries.unknownBehavior: On anchor_evaluations, UNKNOWN records remain UNKNOWN.
consumers: available for future relation coverage plans
limitations: Agent-visible only on anchor_evaluations until broader collection semantics are designed and tested.

entries.description: Approved recipe for wide entry, defending block shift toward ball side, and post-anchor outcome classification.
entries.unknownBehavior: unknown_evidence_policy=exclude_candidate.
limitations: Approved plan relies on a legacy compatibility profile for exact M1 parity.
Several high-level stages are still represented as primitives.
entries.visibilityNote: Approved reviewed recipe suitable as user tactical vocabulary.

entries.description: Experimental recipe that finds geometric progressive corridor availability from possession anchors.
entries.unknownBehavior: unknown_evidence_policy=exclude_candidate; relation anchor coverage preserves UNKNOWN before filtering.
limitations: Experimental, not an approved tactical family.
It is geometric corridor availability, not pass probability or decision quality.
entries.visibilityNote: Experimental recipe artifact; usable by Hermes as a reviewed example/composition target, not an approved user-facing promise.

entries.description: Experimental recipe composing block shift, opposite-side corridor detection, and destination-entry classification.
entries.temporalBehavior: sequence of anchor, relation episode, and post-relation destination entry
entries.unknownBehavior: unknown_evidence_policy=exclude_candidate.
limitations: Still inherits recipe-specific block-shift and outcome stages.
Does not prove a weak-side pass was optimal, intended, or available under body orientation/offside constraints.
entries.visibilityNote: Experimental recipe artifact; usable by Hermes as a reviewed example/composition target, not an approved user-facing promise.

entries.description: Compute distance from a candidate player/point to a progressive corridor segment inside the geometric corridor relation.
entries.unknownBehavior: Not exposed as a standalone catalog capability; enclosing relation is UNKNOWN when required points or segment endpoints are unavailable.
limitations: Compiler lowering only; not user-facing tactical vocabulary by itself.
entries.visibilityNote: Internal geometric kernel; do not expose as recipe-discovery vocabulary.

entries.description: Assign destination-side/lane evidence inside the geometric corridor relation.
entries.unknownBehavior: Enclosing relation is UNKNOWN or absent when lane assignment cannot be computed from required geometry.
limitations: Embedded relation evidence; general lane occupancy is a future capability, not this internal helper.

entries.description: Resolve destination-region bounds for corridor and destination-entry classification.
entries.unknownBehavior: Enclosing relation or classifier cannot prove entry when bounds cannot be resolved.
limitations: Compiler/relation lowering detail; not a standalone user-facing recipe concept.

duplicatesOrAdapters: geometric_progressive_corridor and geometric_progressive_corridor_from_anchor_set duplicate football semantics with different input contracts.
wide_channel_dwell and shift_persistence are legacy documented primitives now represented by predicate/operator composition.
docs/primitives/registry.yaml lists robust_team_width and robust_team_length, but they are not current catalog entries.
mislayeredOrOverstated: signed_lateral_shift is registered as a primitive but embeds candidate extraction, baseline/search policy, quality gating, and anchor creation.
outcome_classification is recipe-specific L4 logic registered as a primitive.
geometric_progressive_corridor is agent-visible and useful, but its L1 geometric lowering is not cataloged as reusable kernel operations.
hiddenDependencies: possession_segment reads minimum_possession_seconds and analysis_rate_hz from recipe parameters although the catalog entry declares no parameters.

signed_lateral_shift reads baseline_window_seconds, shift_search_window_seconds, minimum_outfield_players_per_team, prior_central_fraction, and minimum_wide_dwell_seconds through shared runtime state rather than catalog-declared node parameters.
outcome_classification reads result_id_seed_hash, analysis_rate_hz, dedupe_window_seconds, opposite_side_fraction, and retained_after_switch_seconds beyond its declared horizon parameter.
geometric corridor implementations depend on analysis_rate_hz from shared runtime parameters in addition to node parameters.

tacticalKnowledgePack.reconciliation: The external review referenced 44a9b5fb3f3748c0e8a7bc80a4134fe3cb062dd66807ca20a671cb980529b7c6, which is recorded in delivery/ledger.jsonl and docs/reviews/2026-06-21-m1-2-s2i-a-local-verification.md for an earlier S2I-A local-verification state. The clean committed source snapshot audited here is 7ec9163a6d3fcd2e1c4f197f1a2d8da24de44341, where generated/tactical-knowledge-pack.json hashes to 28405b82d54b961459842d77f1547e3a37fa5bb1ce2b7435a8a919732f804854. This regenerated audit treats 28405b82d54b961459842d77f1547e3a37fa5bb1ce2b7435a8a919732f804854 as the exact audited pack hash and records 44a9b5fb3f3748c0e8a7bc80a4134fe3cb062dd66807ca20a671cb980529b7c6 as the historical review-reference hash.

visibilityTierLegend.USER_TACTICAL: Named concepts appropriate for direct user-facing recipes.
visibilityTierLegend.AGENT_COMPOSABLE: Safe building blocks Hermes may use in experimental plans after their contracts are complete.
visibilityTierLegend.COMPILER_LOWERING: Low-level geometry, kinematics, predicate, or source-fact lowering vocabulary not normally shown in recipe discovery.
visibilityTierLegend.TRUSTED_RECIPE_ONLY: High-level bundled logic usable only inside a reviewed recipe until hidden dependencies are formalized or split.
visibilityTierLegend.LEGACY_DOCUMENTATION: Historical or compatibility-only behavior, not a current authoring target.

standardLibraryExtensionChecklist: at least one real tactical consumer

hiddenDependencyTreatments.note: Formalize all shared runtime parameters in the catalog contract before broad direct Hermes exposure; existing reviewed recipes may keep supplying them at recipe level.
Keep restricted until its actual detector-stage inputs and selection policy are explicit, or split it into lower-level reusable capabilities.
Keep restricted because this is high-level M1 recipe logic, not a general primitive contract.
Make shared timing and internal geometric lowering dependencies explicit without exposing raw coordinate internals as user-facing recipe vocabulary.
Document this as an input-contract adapter for the same relation, not a separate football concept.

contractExposureRule: Capabilities with hidden dependencies must either formalize all actual inputs and parameters in the contract or be marked TRUSTED_RECIPE_ONLY. Incomplete contracts must not be exposed to Hermes as general authoring vocabulary.
